joke: Why did the marketer break up with the CRM? It kept sending mixed signals.
How many martech vendors does it take to change a lightbulb? Just one, but they'll upsell you the smart bulb, the IoT hub, and a 12-month analytics subscription.
A CDP, a DMP, and a MAP walk into a bar. The bartender says, 'We don't serve your kind here.' They say, 'That's okay, we'll just segment the audience and retarget you later.'
Why did the email marketer get therapy? Too many unresolved open issues.
I asked our marketing automation platform to plan my vacation. It sent me a 7-step nurture sequence and a lead scoring model.
What do you call a marketer who only uses vanity metrics? An influencer.
Our A/B test came back inconclusive. We're now running an A/B/C/D/E/F test — we call it the 'alphabet campaign.'
Why don't martech stacks ever get lonely? Because they're always integrated.
A customer walked into a store. The personalization engine showed them 47 ads for the shoes they already bought. Classic.
What's a marketer's favorite horror movie? 'The Silence of the Churn.'
Why did the attribution model go to therapy? It had serious last-touch issues.
Our marketing stack has 47 tools. We use 3 of them. The rest are on a 'strategic roadmap.'
What do you call a marketer obsessed with funnels? Someone who really needs to get out of the TOFU.
I told our data team I needed customer insights. They sent me a dashboard with 200 metrics and the message: 'You figure it out.'
Why did the CMO cry at the board meeting? The ROI attribution model gave all the credit to direct traffic. Again.
What's the difference between a martech vendor and a magician? The magician eventually reveals the trick.
Our CDP unified the customer view so well, it now knows our customers better than they know themselves. Unfortunately, they still won't buy anything.
Why did the programmatic ad get rejected? It failed the brand safety check for being too on-brand.

A marketer died and went to heaven. St. Peter said, 'We have good news and bad news.' The marketer said, 'Tell me the bad news first.' St. Peter replied, 'Your email open rate was 12%.' The marketer said, 'What's the good news?' St. Peter said, 'Industry average is 11%.'
Why did the SEO specialist break up with the social media manager? There was no engagement.
